about.title: What is ROBOTIKAI?
paragraphs: ROBOTIKAI was designed as a platform that gathers both education and hardware needs under the same roof in the field of Arduino, robotics, and software. We aim to provide practical content for students, teachers, and enthusiasts.

The platform structure is built upon online courses, project-ready kits, blog posts, event announcements, and a powerful admin panel. Thus, you can manage both content and products from a single place.
This page is currently for promotional purposes. In the future, you can make sections like mission, vision, instructor team, collaborations dynamic via the admin panel.

contact.intro: For courses, corporate collaborations, or bulk kit purchases, you can fill out the form below or use our contact information.
contact.infoText: The information here is a placeholder. We will make your address, phone, and e-mail address manageable from the admin panel.

courses.title: Arduino, robotics, and coding courses
courses.intro: We will list all your educational content here, from basic courses for beginners to project-oriented advanced modules. You are seeing sample courses for now; you will be able to manage these from the panel when the admin panel arrives.

items.title: Arduino 0 to 1 Beginner
items.highlight: Fundamentals of LEDs, buttons, and sensors.
items.content: In this training, you will get to know the Arduino board, build LED, button, and sensor circuits on a breadboard, and write basic C codes. Designed for beginners with weekly live lessons and application videos for a total of 6 weeks.

items.title: Intermediate Robotics Projects
items.content: You will realize line following and obstacle avoiding robot projects step by step. We will focus on motor drivers, ultrasonic sensors, and PID control logic. A project-based, practical program.

items.title: IoT and Cloud with Arduino
items.content: Sending data over WiFi with ESP8266/ESP32, MQTT protocol, and cloud dashboard integration. All the necessary tools for smart home and remote monitoring projects.

shop.title: Arduino kits and robotics components
shop.intro: Project-ready sets and sensors that work integrated with the trainings. You are seeing sample products for now; you will add and manage actual products from the admin panel.

items.name: ROBOTIKAI Beginner Arduino Kit
items.info: Complete set with LEDs, resistors, buttons, buzzer, and basic sensors.
items.description: A complete starter set including Arduino Uno R3, breadboard, jumper cables, LEDs, resistors, buttons, buzzer, LDR, and temperature sensor. Works compatibly with the trainings.

items.name: Line Following Robot Kit
items.info: Motor driver, DC motors, line sensors, and chassis.
items.description: With L298N motor driver, 2 DC motors, 3-sensor line follower module, and ready chassis, you can immediately assemble and start coding.

items.name: Smart Home IoT Kit
items.info: WiFi module, relay board, temp/humidity sensor, light sensor.
items.description: ESP8266 NodeMCU, 4-channel relay module, DHT22 temp/humidity sensor, and LDR. Ready set for smart home and remote control projects.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: What you wonder about Arduino, courses, and orders.

items.question: When will my Arduino kit order be shipped?
items.answer: Your order will be shipped within 1-2 business days after it is confirmed. The tracking number will be sent via e-mail.

items.question: How can I register for the courses?
items.answer: You can register by clicking the register button on the relevant course page or by contacting us via our contact form.

items.question: Which payment methods are accepted?
items.answer: You can pay with credit card and debit card via our secure payment infrastructure.

blog.title: Articles on Arduino, robotics, and coding
blog.intro: Technical articles, project descriptions, and advice supporting the educational content. You are seeing sample articles for now; you will add actual blog posts from the admin panel.

items.title: What is Arduino? Explained from Scratch
items.excerpt: A simple introduction to the Arduino board structure, pins, power supply, and basic components.
items.content: Arduino is an open-source electronics development platform. Thanks to microcontroller-based boards, an easy programming environment, and a library compatible with many sensors/motors, you can quickly bring robotics and IoT projects to life. In this article, we offer a simple introduction to pins, power supply, and basic components.

items.title: How Does a Line Following Robot Work?
items.excerpt: We discuss sensor placement, speed adjustment, PID logic, and example code flow.
items.content: Line following robots move autonomously thanks to sensors that detect the line on the floor. Sensor placement, motor speed adjustment, and PID control logic are critical parameters. In this article, you will find step-by-step example code and debugging tips.

items.title: Top 5 Most Useful Arduino Projects for Students
items.excerpt: Project ideas most commonly used in schools and courses that accelerate learning.
items.content: Projects like LED blinking, traffic light, parking sensor, temperature monitor, and simple robotic arm are both fun and instructive. Each reinforces different concepts: timing, sensor reading, motor control, and logical decisions.
